title: saussurea recurvata
intro: Saussurea recurvata, also known as the Inula or Inula hookeri, is a plant species in the daisy family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and acting as an expectorant, making it easier to expel mucus from the lungs. In herbal preparations, Saussurea recurvata is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
